title: Lesson Quiz: Job Outlook and Salary Analysis
description: Assessment of the concept that Demand, growth trends, and earning potential.

questions.prompt: What does job outlook information describe?

choices.text: Whether a career is expected to grow, stay stable, or decline
choices.feedback: Correct. Job outlook information helps students understand future growth or change in a career field.
Favorite subjects may connect to career interests, but they are not outlook information.
Personal strengths are different from job outlook data.
A dress code is not part of job outlook information.

questions.prompt: Salary information can help students compare career options.

choices.feedback: Salary information is useful because it helps students compare career options.
Correct. Salary information is one factor students can use when comparing careers.

questions.prompt: What does demand mean in career research?

choices.text: How much employers need workers in a career field
choices.feedback: This idea is related, but it does not define demand as clearly as the correct answer.

choices.text: How many assignments students complete in one week
choices.feedback: Demand in this lesson refers to careers, not school assignments.

choices.text: The level of need for workers in a career area
choices.feedback: Correct. Demand refers to how strongly workers are needed in a field.
A daily schedule is not the same as demand.

questions.prompt: Why should students use outlook and salary data when exploring careers?

choices.text: Because career data should replace personal interests completely
choices.feedback: Career data is important, but it should not replace personal interests and goals.

choices.text: Because the data helps students compare careers using real information
choices.feedback: This idea is related, but it does not best emphasize informed comparison.

choices.text: Because data should be ignored once a career sounds interesting
choices.feedback: Students should keep using evidence even when a career sounds interesting.

choices.text: Because the data helps students make more informed comparisons between options
choices.feedback: Correct. Outlook and salary data give students evidence they can use to compare career options.

questions.prompt: Job outlook and salary information should be the only factors used in choosing a career.

choices.feedback: This is incorrect. Job outlook and salary matter, but students should also consider personal fit and pathway requirements.
Correct. Career choices should include more than outlook and salary alone.
